items.title: GNFS intensifies fire safety education in Eastern Region communities
items.description: The Ghana National Fire Service (GNFS) has intensified fire safety education campaigns across communities in the Eastern Region as part of efforts to reduce fire outbreaks and promote public awareness on prevention measures. Personnel from the Anyinam Fire Station engaged residents and shop owners in Akyem Anyinam, educating them on fire prevention, emergency response, and the safe use of fire extinguishers and liquefied petroleum gas (LPG). The team cautioned the public against unsafe practices, including illegal electrical connections, overloading of sockets and improper storage of flammable materials. Residents were also encouraged to report fire incidents promptly and obtain fire permits before undertaking construction projects. At Nkawkaw Amanfrom, the Visibility Team of the Nkawkaw Fire Station conducted a doorstep campaign targeting shop owners, traders, food vendors and mobile money operators. The engagement focused on basic fire safety practices, emergency response measures and ways individuals and businesses can minimise fire risks. Meanwhile, the Aduamoa Fire Station, led by Leading Fireman (LFm) Debrah, educated residents, construction workers and traders at the Kwahu Tafo Bokoruwa Roundabout on LPG safety, agrochemical handling, electrical safety, good housekeeping and basic emergency response. The GNFS said the exercises recorded high public participation, with many residents demonstrating improved awareness of fire prevention measures and committing to adopt safer practices. Participants were also encouraged to keep fire extinguishers where necessary and promptly report fire outbreaks to the GNFS to enable quick response and minimise damage.

items.title: Nigeria, Germany pledge stronger trade, energy, security cooperation
items.description: By Favour Ulebor, Abuja Nigeria and Germany have reaffirmed their commitment to deepening bilateral relations, with both countries pledging stronger cooperation in trade, investment, energy, security, education and other strategic areas. The commitment was made on Wednesday in Abuja during a bilateral meeting between Nigeria’s Minister of Foreign Affairs, Ambassador Bianca Odumegwu-Ojukwu, and the German Federal Minister for Foreign Affairs, Johann Wadephul. Odumegwu-Ojukwu described Germany as one of Nigeria’s most important strategic partners in Europe, noting that relations between both countries had grown over decades through mutual respect, trust and cooperation. She said the partnership extends beyond diplomacy to include trade and investment, development cooperation, renewable energy, education, vocational training, science and technology, migration, culture, peace and security. The minister said Nigeria was committed to expanding collaboration with Germany in emerging sectors such as renewable energy, digital innovation, artificial intelligence, critical minerals, manufacturing, agriculture, healthcare, infrastructure development and the green economy. She identified the Nigeria-Germany Bi-national Commission as a key platform for advancing bilateral cooperation, noting that the successful 2025 session of the commission reaffirmed both countries’ resolve to expand partnerships across priority areas. Odumegwu-Ojukwu said Nigeria had continued to implement agreements reached through the commission by pursuing economic reforms aimed at improving the investment climate, strengthening governance, encouraging private sector participation and promoting sustainable growth. She also commended Germany’s support to the Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS), particularly in the areas of regional integration, democratic governance, peacebuilding, conflict prevention, humanitarian assistance and institutional development. The minister welcomed Germany’s continued partnership with ECOWAS in addressing terrorism, violent extremism, unconstitutional changes of government and transnational organised crime in West Africa. On economic relations, she urged increased participation by German companies in Nigeria’s priority sectors, including renewable energy, manufacturing, critical minerals, transport infrastructure, agriculture, digital technology and industrial development. Responding, Wadephul said the relationship between Nigeria and Germany had remained strategic for more than 65 years, recalling that Germany established its embassy in Lagos shortly after Nigeria gained independence in 1960. The German minister, who was accompanied by senior officials and business representatives, described Nigeria as Germany’s biggest trading partner in Europe, adding that trade volume between both countries increased by 10 per cent last year. He said the Nigeria-Germany Business Forum would continue to serve as a platform for strengthening trade and investment ties, adding that his visit would encourage more German investors to explore opportunities in Nigeria. Wadephul also expressed Germany’s interest in Nigeria’s energy sector, noting that the Nigeria-Germany Bi-national Commission had continued to provide an effective framework for cooperation. He reiterated Germany’s commitment to supporting ECOWAS in tackling insecurity in the region, particularly terrorism and violent extremism in the Sahel. The German foreign minister pledged to work closely with his Nigerian counterpart to further strengthen the strategic partnership between both countries. The post Nigeria, Germany pledge stronger trade, energy, security cooperation appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: UI appoints Prof Olapegba as 14th substantive VC
items.description: By Adeola Badru The Governing Council of the University of Ibadan has appointed Professor Peter Olamakinde Olapegba as the institution’s 14th substantive Vice-Chancellor following a competitive selection process involving eight candidates. The appointment was announced on Wednesday by the Pro-Chancellor and Chairman of the Governing Council, Chief Adebisi Akande, shortly after the council approved the recommendation of the Joint Council-Senate Selection Board. Announcing the appointment, Akande said the process of selecting a new vice-chancellor commenced in line with the provisions of the University Act ahead of the expiration of the tenure of the incumbent vice-chancellor on November 1, 2026. He explained that advertisements inviting applications for the position were published in three national newspapers in April 2026, attracting six applications from qualified candidates. According to him, the governing council subsequently constituted a search team, as required by law, to identify other suitably qualified academics who had not initially applied for the position. The exercise yielded two additional applications, bringing the total number of candidates to eight. Akande disclosed that the council met with all eight candidates on Tuesday, July 21, for a rigorous assessment during which members independently evaluated and scored each candidate in accordance with approved guidelines. He said the exercise produced five candidates who were shortlisted in order of merit for the final stage of the selection process. Akande further explained that the Governing Council appointed two of its members to serve on the Joint Council-Senate Selection Board, while the University Senate had earlier elected two representatives to participate in the exercise. He noted that the selection board interviewed the five shortlisted candidates on Wednesday before recommending Professor Olapegba as the most suitable candidate for the office of vice-chancellor. Describing the selection process as “thorough, transparent and highly competitive”, Akande said the Governing Council unanimously approved the recommendation of the Selection Board. “It is, therefore, my pleasure to present to you the 14th Vice-Chancellor of this great university, the University of Ibadan, Professor Peter Olamakinde Olapegba. We wish him success in his tenure,” Akande said. Professor Olapegba, who currently serves as the university’s deputy vice-chancellor (administration) and is a professor in the Department of Psychology, will succeed the incumbent vice-chancellor, whose tenure expires on November 1, 2026. His appointment makes him the 14th substantive vice-chancellor of Nigeria’s premier university following a competitive selection process conducted in accordance with the university’s statutory provisions. The post UI appoints Prof Olapegba as 14th substantive VC appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Here’s the latest.
BoG, GoldBod finalising new financing model to reduce pressure on central bank balance sheet
items.description: The Bank of Ghana (BoG) and the Ghana Gold Board (GoldBod) are finalising a new financing arrangement aimed at supporting the country’s gold purchasing programme while reducing pressure on the central bank’s balance sheet. Speaking after the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting, Bank of Ghana Governor Dr. Johnson Pandit Asiama said discussions with GoldBod are ongoing, adding that a decision on the new funding model will be announced soon. “We are still in discussions with them. From where we are now, GoldBod has a number of choices. Either government takes up its funding entirely, or a resort to the markets to fund it. We are still discussing that context with the Gold Board, and I’m sure the market will be informed very soon,” he said. According to the Governor, regardless of the financing option eventually adopted, the Bank of Ghana will continue to play a role in supporting the country’s gold accumulation programme. Responding to a question on whether the new arrangement would complement the Bank’s existing gold purchase programme, Dr. Asiama responded in the affirmative. He explained that the proposed changes form part of broader reforms designed to protect the Bank of Ghana’s financial position while preserving the benefits of the domestic gold purchase programme. “Remember last year, the impact on our financials was quite significant. Since then, a number of steps have been taken, and this change is actually part of the reforms so that we continue to benefit from the activities of GoldBod, but at the same time, we stem the impact on the central bank’s balance sheet,” the Governor stated. The comments suggest the central bank is seeking a more sustainable funding model for GoldBod’s operations as Ghana expands its domestic gold purchasing programme. The Governor indicated that the preferred financing structure is expected to balance the need to support gold purchases with efforts to safeguard the Bank of Ghana’s balance sheet and maintain macroeconomic stability. The Bank of Ghana is expected to provide further details once consultations with GoldBod and other stakeholders are concluded.

items.title: Gunmen kill Rivers vigilante leader, three women in Omoku
items.description: By Davies Iheamnachor PORT HARCOURT — Unidentified gunmen have killed the leader of a local vigilante group, alongside three women, in Omoku, headquarters of Ogba/Egbema/Ndoni Local Government Area of Rivers State. The incident, which occurred on Monday evening along Oba Road in Omoku, claimed the life of Ozomela Stephen Nwaocha, the Omoku Zonal Commander of the ONELGA Security Planning and Advisory Committee (OSPAC). Sources said the attack threw residents into panic, forcing many to flee for safety, while business owners hurriedly closed their shops. The Public Relations Officer of OSPAC, Comrade Nkem Godknows, said the gunmen attacked Nwaocha while he was relaxing with his sister and her friend near his residence. He said the assailants also shot three women, who died at the scene, while two other victims sustained gunshot injuries and were receiving treatment at a hospital. “The incident happened a few minutes past 5 p.m. Our commander was with his sister and her friend, seated around his neighbourhood, when the unknown gunmen approached and shot him dead. “They also shot the girls. The three women died on the spot, while two other victims sustained gunshot wounds and are currently receiving treatment at the hospital. The police were immediately informed, and we reported the incident without delay,” he said. Godknows appealed to residents of Omoku to remain calm, assuring that OSPAC, in collaboration with other security agencies, would work to unravel those behind the attack. He also appealed for greater support for members of OSPAC, saying the group lacked adequate welfare and financial assistance. “We are not adequately taken care of. We are not paid, and we have no reliable source of income. We have little or no support or encouragement. I am calling on the general public to support OSPAC,” he said. Meanwhile, normalcy has reportedly returned to the area, with security operatives conducting patrols to maintain peace and order. The Rivers State Police Command condemned the attack, promising to ensure that the perpetrators are arrested and brought to justice. The Police Public Relations Officer, Blessing Agabe, an Assistant Superintendent of Police, said the command was saddened by the incident and appealed to residents to provide useful information that could aid investigations. “The Rivers State Command is saddened by this incident, which is disturbing the relative peace being enjoyed in the state. The command condemns this act entirely and reiterates that the perpetrators will be brought to justice,” she said. The post Gunmen kill Rivers vigilante leader, three women in Omoku appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Lutte contre le grand banditisme en milieu urbain : La Police Nationale démantèle deux réseaux de malfaiteurs à Ouagadougou
items.description: Dans le cadre de la lutte contre le grand banditisme sous toutes ses formes, le Service Régional de la Police Judiciaire (SRPJ) du Kadiogo a effectué de minutieuses enquêtes ayant abouti à l’identification puis au démantèlement de deux réseaux de malfaiteurs, spécialisés dans des agressions, vols à main armée et cambriolages de domiciles et lieux de commerce. Ils opéraient dans plusieurs quartiers de la capitale tels que Samandin, Pissy, Gounghin et à Kokologho. Le mode opératoire des deux groupes, quasiment identique, consistait d’abord en un repérage ou un ciblage des lieux de commerce, des domiciles et des magasins. Ils choisissaient ensuite un moment propice de la journée ou de la nuit et passaient à l’action. A l’aide d’armes à feu, ils tenaient en respect leurs victimes et les dépossédaient de tous leurs objets de valeur (engins à deux roues, ordinateurs, téléphones portables et numéraire). Il arrivait des fois qu’ils opérent dans des quartiers communément appelés non lotis où ils s’introduisaient dans des domiciles la journée au moment où les occupants des lieux rejoignaient leurs lieux de travail. Les enquêtes savamment menées avec l’appui de collaborations ont permis d’appréhender les auteurs et de saisir deux pistolets automatiques et des munitions, des armes blanches, des motos, des téléphones portables, des pièces détachées de moto, des vélos pour enfants et divers autres objets. La Police Nationale remercie l’ensemble des populations, particulièrement les responsables des initiatives locales de sécurité, pour leur collaboration qui a permis d’atteindre une fois de plus ces résultats. Elle les exhorte, par la même occasion, à plus de vigilance et à continuer dans la même dynamique de dénonciation des cas suspects aux numéros verts que sont le 17, le 16 et le 1010. La Police Nationale, une force publique au service des citoyens !

items.title: LCCI seeks stronger regulatory oversight to curb exploitative pricing
items.description: By Yinka Kolawole The Lagos Chamber of Commerce and Industry (LCCI) has called for stronger regulatory oversight of Nigeria’s pricing environment, urging the Federal Competition and Consumer Protection Commission (FCCPC) and other relevant agencies to protect consumers and businesses from unfair market practices. Speaking on the state of the economy in Lagos, Wednesday, LCCI President, Engr. Leye Kupoluyi, said while the chamber does not advocate price controls, regulators should ensure that pricing reflects prevailing market realities in line with international best practices. He noted that businesses and consumers have continued to express concern over the persistent asymmetry in domestic petrol pricing, where increases in global crude oil prices are immediately reflected at the pump, while corresponding declines are rarely passed on to consumers. According to him, the trend raises questions about market transparency, pricing fairness and consumer protection. “We call on FCCPC, the Nigerian Midstream and Downstream Petroleum Regulatory Authority (NMDPRA), and other relevant agencies to strengthen oversight of pricing mechanisms, ensure greater transparency, and protect consumers from exploitative market practices,” he said. Kupoluyi also cited cases in the aviation sector where local airlines allegedly fix fares during peak festive periods, arguing that such practices deserve closer regulatory scrutiny. “We have had cases where oil marketers increased pump prices in line with international crude oil prices but retained the same prices when global prices declined. Similar concerns have been raised over airline ticket pricing during festive seasons. These market dynamics should be carefully examined to ensure consumers receive fair value,” he said. The LCCI president stressed that effective regulation should promote competition and discourage anti-competitive practices rather than impose price controls. He maintained that a transparent and competitive pricing framework would boost investor confidence, protect consumers and improve the operating environment for businesses, while ensuring markets function efficiently and fairly. Kupoluyi further advocated reforms to improve the business environment, including harmonising product registration requirements to eliminate multiple approvals for products with identical formulations packaged in different sizes. The post LCCI seeks stronger regulatory oversight to curb exploitative pricing appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Michelin-starred restaurateur faces jail for using ants as dessert garnish
items.description: South Korean prosecutors sought a one-year jail term for the owner of a Michelin-starred restaurant accused of using ants as a dessert garnish, local media reported. Ants are not among the 10 insect species approved for human consumption under the country’s laws. The approved list includes grasshoppers, locusts and two-spotted crickets, among others. Prosecutors on Monday also asked that the restaurant, which holds two Michelin stars, be fined 20 million Korean won ($13,510; £10,100). The owner admitted to serving ants but argued that they were used in a “small part” of the restaurant’s 15-course menu – as a topping for sorbets – and that diners were told they could opt for non-ant toppings. The case came to light after officials from the Ministry of Food and Drug Safety found reviews that diners had posted online with photographs of dishes sprinkled with ants. Investigators estimate that some 49,000 ants, imported from the US and Thailand, were used in several dishes over four years. Health authorities also found that the restaurant used ants that “contained up to 55 times more heavy metals than typical edible insects”, according to a report on The Chosun Daily. South Korean authorities consider several criteria in deciding whether insects can be approved as food. These include food safety risks, toxicity, nutritional value, and whether they can be bred and processed hygienically. In its defence the restaurant owner argued that just 60% of diners had opted to try the ants, while the rest were served alternatives like fermented vinegar and edible flowers. He added that restaurants in other countries, such as Australia, Denmark and the UK, also use ants as ingredients. Court documents did not name the restaurant nor its owner. Ten restaurants in South Korea, all located in capital city Seoul, have been awarded two Michelin stars. The Michelin Guide awards up to three stars to eateries based on ingredient quality, flavour mastery, cooking technique, chef’s personality and consistency. Two stars denote restaurants that do “excellent cooking” and are “worth a detour”, according to Michelin’s criteria. The court is set to deliver its verdict on September 2. Ants are eaten, sometimes even valued as a delicacy, in several Asian, African and Latin American cultures. Weaver ants and their eggs are used in Thai and Cambodian cooking, while in Colombia, queen ants are as prized as caviar.

items.title: Burkina : Le Gouvernement lève l’interdiction sur l’exportation de la farine de céréales
items.description: Le Ministère en charge du Commerce a annoncé le mardi 21 juillet 2026 à Ouagadougou, la levée de la suspension de l'exportation de la farine de céréales (mil, maïs et sorgho) sur l'ensemble du territoire national. C'était dans un communiqué adressé aux commerçants exportateurs et à l'ensemble de la population. La mesure prend effet à compter de la date de signature du nouveau communiqué ministériel. Cette décision met fin à la suspension instaurée par le communiqué n°022-0001/MDICAPME/SG du 23 février 2022, qui interdisait l'exportation de la farine de céréales (mil, maïs et sorgho). Selon le communiqué, cette levée vise principalement à favoriser l'écoulement des stocks de produits dérivés des céréales détenus par les unités nationales de transformation. Toutefois, le Gouvernement rappelle que l'exportation de la farine de céréales demeure strictement encadrée. Les opérateurs économiques souhaitant exporter ces produits sont tenus d'obtenir au préalable une Autorisation Spéciale d'Exportation (ASE), conformément à la réglementation en vigueur. Le ministère avertit également que tout manquement à cette obligation expose les contrevenants aux sanctions prévues par les textes en vigueur. Minute.bf

items.title: Keyamo, Ganduje inspect upgraded Hajj Terminal, FAAN raises capacity
items.description: By Dickson Omobola Federal Airports Authority of Nigeria, FAAN, has disclosed that the newly renovated Hajj Terminal at the Murtala Muhammed International Airport, MMIA, Lagos, can process between 8,500 and 10,000 passengers during the Hajj season. Managing Director of FAAN, Mrs Olubunmi Kuku, said the terminal had become dilapidated over the years and no longer offered a pleasant experience for pilgrims. She spoke, Wednesday, during an inspection of the facility, where the Minister of Aviation and Aerospace Development, Mr Festus Keyamo, said the renovation was aimed at ensuring that Nigerian pilgrims enjoy a seamless Hajj exercise. Keyamo said: “From the outset, President Bola Tinubu has been clear about the revitalisation of infrastructure within the aviation sector. You can see the level of attention the President has given to the sector. You can see the ongoing revamping of airports, the reconstruction of the Lagos airport, the resurfacing of the Kano runway and several other projects. “This is just one in a series of steps that the President has mandated us to take. The redevelopment of the Hajj Terminal here is aimed at ensuring that our pilgrims enjoy, as much as possible, a seamless Hajj exercise every year. This is not limited to Lagos; we are going to Kano next week and to other parts of the country. We are undertaking similar projects across the country. “Within the limits of the resources available to us, you can see how much has been achieved here. You went inside with us and saw the prayer room, the VIP section, the general boarding area and the screening machines. “I don’t think this facility falls short of our standards anywhere. Within the limited resources available, and I am sure you know how prudent this government has been in managing resources in the aviation sector, you can see the extent of the improvements that have been made here. “So, for us, and for me personally, we are very impressed. We are very impressed with what we have seen here and with the improvements that have been made to the Hajj Terminal.” For his part, Chairman of FAAN Board, Dr Abdullahi Ganduje, expressed delight at the infrastructure, saying it was an indication of the progressive steps being taken in the aviation sector. Ganduje said: “They say that it is easy to declare a war, but it is difficult to wage it. What I mean is that it is easy to make a policy, but to implement it effectively is very critical. But we are lucky that we have a progressive Minister. He is carrying out the Renewed Hope Agenda perfectly. So, seeing is believing. We are here to inspect the rehabilitation, the renovation, the upgrading of this Hajj Terminal and we are convinced.” The post Keyamo, Ganduje inspect upgraded Hajj Terminal, FAAN raises capacity appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: The evidence that jailed Wontumi was built by NPP – Mustapha Gbande
items.description: Deputy General Secretary of the National Democratic Congress (NDC), Mustapha Gbande, has asserted that the evidence that led to the conviction of New Patriotic Party (NPP) Ashanti Regional Chairman Bernard Antwi Boasiako, popularly known as Chairman Wontumi, was assembled under the previous NPP administration. Speaking on Joy Prime on Wednesday, July 22, Mr Gbande dismissed suggestions that Wontumi’s imprisonment was politically motivated, insisting that it was the culmination of investigations and legal processes initiated by officials of the former Akufo-Addo administration. “The docket that jailed Wontumi, the evidence that jailed him, was built by the NPP. Trust me, the docket that jailed Wontumi, the evidence that jailed him was built by the NPP,” he said. According to Mr Gbande, the case originated in July 2022 , when the then Minister for Lands and Natural Resources declared Wontumi’s mining activities illegal. “It started when the ministers took action against Wontumi. In July 2022, the Minister for Lands declared Wontumi’s activities illegal. So if something is illegal, it’s illegal. If it’s against the law, it’s against the law. For everything that breaches the law, there have to be consequences.” He argued that the case demonstrates that no one, irrespective of political affiliation, is above the law. Mr Gbande said he did not take pleasure in Wontumi’s incarceration but was encouraged that Ghana’s justice system had been allowed to function without political interference. “I think that, for us as Christians, you don’t celebrate anybody’s agony or misfortune. However, we ourselves as a country have submitted our authority to laws. We are all governed by laws, and when the laws are seemingly working, we must embrace it, and we must be happy that justice is in there.” He added that while he was not happy about Wontumi’s imprisonment, he was pleased that the law had been allowed to take its course. Mr Gbande stressed that political parties should never become sanctuaries for individuals who break the law. “Political parties should not be used as a hub to harness lawlessness and insubordinate our laws. Political parties should be a vehicle of prosperity, a platform for nation-building and growth.” Rejecting assertions that Wontumi’s conviction amounted to political persecution, Mr Gbande insisted that the NPP chairman was serving a sentence because of his actions rather than his political affiliation. “I believe that Wontumi is never a political prisoner. He’s just a prisoner as much as a person who has stolen a goat or the people who are in jail. It can happen to any of us. That is why we must work being careful.” He also alleged that some senior figures within the NPP were privately more pleased about Wontumi’s imprisonment than the opposition NDC. “They (NPP) are more excited for Wontumi’s jail than the NDC,” he said. Mr Gbande claimed the party’s leadership had opposed Wontumi’s reported ambition to contest for the position of National Chairman. “The General Secretary of the NPP is one of the people who are happy. The National Chairman of the NPP, they are all happy that Wontumi is no more. They didn’t want him to become National Chairman. It was going to be a nuisance for the party.” Mr Gbande, however, dismissed the NPP’s public show of support for Wontumi, insisting that senior figures within the party were only pretending to stand by him. “They are pretending; the NPP is behaving as people who attend funerals,” he stated. Mr Gbande further argued that Wontumi could have reduced public criticism by acknowledging responsibility for his mining activities and undertaking land reclamation. “If Wontumi had gone back to Samreboi, as a concession owner under the NPP, to declare reclamation and possibly tell the whole country, ‘Listen, I take responsibility for the activities,’ that would have demonstrated remorse.” He noted that although the Akufo-Addo administration had introduced legislation permitting mining in forest reserves, concession holders remained bound by the law. “The president passed a law that allowed people to go and mine in the forest reserves. It wasn’t his fault that he had to mine in the forest because the presidency of President Akufo-Addo put in place a law that allowed people to mine in the forest reserves. He happened to be one person who was given a concession to mine there. But he mined without complying with the laws.” Mr Gbande said Wontumi’s continued insistence that he had done nothing wrong undermined any sympathy he might have received. “The nation had expressed its distaste about what you had done. You should have tried to do right by others. But the man says he hasn’t done anything wrong.” He further alleged that Wontumi intimidated even government officials who attempted to challenge his conduct. “He was threatening even his own appointees, even in government, when they were pointing to him that things were wrong. Do we come to political parties to behave like that?” Mr Gbande concluded by alleging that Wontumi’s political ambitions had created significant opposition within his own party and that he underestimated the intensity of internal party competition. “Strategically, Wontumi should not even have dared contesting. Once you put yourself out for a contest, you will have competitors. And when the competition becomes key, people go as far as trying to eliminate you.” He maintained that those celebrating Wontumi’s conviction were not members of the NDC but individuals within the NPP itself. “The people who are more interested, who are jubilating in their hearts, for whom Wontumi is guilty, is the NPP.”

items.title: Customs to begin 100% cargo scanning at Onne Port, eases emergency protocol
items.description: By Daniel Abia, Port Harcourt The Nigeria Customs Service (NCS) has announced plans to commence 100 per cent scanning of imported cargoes, including pharmaceutical products, at the Onne Port in Rivers State as part of efforts to tighten surveillance and prevent the entry of illicit goods into the country. The service said the measure was aimed at tracking dangerous consignments, especially those involving illegal importation of drugs and other prohibited items through the port. The National Public Relations Officer of the NCS, Abdullahi Maiwada, disclosed this during an interactive session with journalists in Port Harcourt on Tuesday ahead of the 2026 Public Relations Week with the theme: “Realigning Corporate Communication as a Strategic Driver of Institutional Effectiveness and Public Trust.” Maiwada explained that all cargoes arriving through the port would undergo pre-scanning, with images stored for future reference and further analysis when necessary. He said the 100 per cent scanning initiative had already commenced at the Apapa Port in Lagos and would soon be extended to Onne due to evolving tactics employed by smugglers. The Customs spokesperson recalled that the Comptroller-General of Customs, Bashir Adeniyi, declared a state of emergency at the Onne Port in September 2024 following the interception of arms, ammunition and illicit drugs at the facility. Adeniyi had announced the introduction of a three-month emergency protocol to enhance scrutiny of cargoes entering the country through the port. Maiwada, however, clarified that the emergency declaration did not halt port operations or importation activities, but was aimed at deploying additional resources and increasing monitoring of activities at the facility. “When we say state of emergency at the Onne Port, it does not mean that the business of clearance will not take place. We are putting more eyes, deploying more resources to make sure that illicit items do not find their way into the port,” he said. He added that Onne Port remained a place of special interest to the service, with increased surveillance and operational attention. “Whether it has been cancelled or not, eyes are still on Onne Port. Our resources are inclined towards airports, and there are so many operations going on that I may not be able to reveal here,” he said. Maiwada, a Deputy Comptroller of Customs, said the service was also strengthening its non-intrusive inspection technology through the deployment of scanners. “It does not mean that we have stopped importation. Another aspect that we are working on is our non-intrusive inspection technology, which is scanning,” he said. He explained that scanned images would be integrated with import declarations as part of measures to improve checks and accountability. “We have started 100 per cent scanning in Apapa, and we have started integration of scanning images with your declaration. Those are checks and balances we are putting in place,” he added. He noted that the introduction of new technology had faced resistance from some quarters but assured that the service remained committed to implementing reforms aimed at protecting Nigerians from harmful imports. “People will resist change. So very soon we are coming to Onne that 100 per cent scanning will also take place, and we’ll make sure that all items that come in are pre-scanned and the images will be stored,” he said. According to him, the next batch of scanners being deployed by the service would include Onne Port among other facilities nationwide. Maiwada said the media engagement was aimed at strengthening collaboration between the Customs Service and journalists to enhance public trust, transparency and awareness of ongoing reforms. He stressed that the media remained a strategic partner in communicating government policies, promoting trade facilitation and supporting national security efforts. The post Customs to begin 100% cargo scanning at Onne Port, eases emergency protocol appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: 21 Ondo lawmakers push to oust speaker over alleged OSOPADEC misconduct
items.description: -This is not about Aiyedatiwa – House spokesperson By Dayo Johnson, Akure Crisis in the Ondo State House of Assembly deepened on Monday as 21 of the 26 members signed in support of the removal of Speaker, Rt. Hon. Olamide Oladiji. The spokesperson of the Assembly, Hon. Olatunji Fabiyi, said the move was an internal decision and had no link with the Executive. This follows allegations by Hon. Olawumi Fayemi-Obayelu, representing Ilaje Constituency II, that N44 million released by the Ondo State Oil Producing Areas Development Commission, OSOPADEC, was mismanaged. Fayemi-Obayelu accused the Speaker of involvement in the alleged mismanagement of funds meant for riverine communities in Ilaje Local Government Area. She also claimed her investigation into the matter had attracted threats to her life, adding that she “narrowly escaped death” recently. Speaking in Akure, the assembly spokesperson, Fabiyi, said the lawmakers had resolved that the Speaker should vacate office, but decided to brief Governor Lucky Aiyedatiwa first to dispel insinuations that the planned removal was being orchestrated by the executive. According to him, “We have resolved that Mr. Speaker should go. But in the wisdom of the leadership of the House, we felt we should meet with Mr. Governor,” Fabiyi said. “We took the resolution of members to him because when we acted previously, people accused us of being sponsored. We told the governor that this has nothing to do with him. ” This is about the Speaker’s conduct. He is the one leading us, and we no longer want him to lead us. ” Twenty-one members have already signed for his removal.” Fabiyi attributed the crisis to the handling of the OSOPADEC budget, alleging that a resolution was prepared and presented as the decision of the House without the knowledge or approval of lawmakers. “It is about the OSOPADEC budget. A resolution was written in the name of members without our knowledge. Fabiyi added that ” It was stated that members had deliberated and agreed to it, whereas we did not. You cannot shave our heads behind our backs. That is what we are fighting against,” he said. He said the governor appealed for more time before lawmakers proceeded with further action. Fabiyi warned that if the Speaker failed to resign after the period requested by the governor, lawmakers would invoke constitutional procedures to remove him from office. On reports that the Speaker was still in possession of the Assembly’s mace, Fabiyi said it would not frustrate their plan. “We are legislators. Don’t teach us our job. We know how to go about it when the time comes,” he said. The post 21 Ondo lawmakers push to oust speaker over alleged OSOPADEC misconduct appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Petrol pump owners defer strike call after talks with petroleum minister
items.description: The All Pakistan Petroleum Pump Owners Association (APPPOA) has deferred its nationwide strike call for two weeks after negotiations with the petroleum minister. A day earlier, the association announced a nationwide strike from Wednesday night after negotiations with the government over daily petroleum pricing failed. Addressing the media, Petroleum Minister Ali Pervaiz Malik said that after another round of negotiations with the APPPOA, it was decided to defer the strike call after the government assured stakeholders that their grievances would be addressed within the next two weeks. “From February 28 till today, the government has used around Rs100 billion to mitigate the impact of rising fuel prices and initiated a subsidy scheme for the most vulnerable section of the society,” said Malik while referring to the actions taken by the government following the outbreak of the Iran-US war . “The clouds of war are again building in the region and fuel prices are rising,” said Malik, adding that he discussed the prevailing situation with APPPOA officials and they “supported“ the government in its efforts. The minister said the country’s top civil and military leadership was in talks with Iranian and US government officials to work towards a resolution of the conflict. The petroleum minister, referring to the grievances of petrol pump owners, said the issue of dealers’ margins would be resolved through consultations with all stakeholders, adding that a summary in this regard would be submitted to the federal cabinet for consideration. He further said that the government would review the daily fuel pricing mechanism with stakeholders from the petroleum sector after two weeks. Last week, the minister announced that fuel prices would now be fixed on a daily basis due to fluctuations in international market prices following renewed hostilities between Iran and the US. During the press conference, Malik said the Oil and Gas Regulatory Authority (Ogra) would start publishing fuel prices on its website from that day and that a breakup of the same would also be provided in Urdu for the sake of transparency. Acknowledging the difficulties faced by the oil sector, Malik said the government has committed to resolving their issues over the next fortnight after consultations with Ogra and other stakeholders. “For some years now, the issue of dealers’ margins has been pending. A progress report and summary will be presented to the cabinet to ensure that those earning honestly can meet their expenses through the margin.” He assured the association officials present at the presser that they would receive good news within the next two weeks. “But the rules we are adopting going forward are to bring transparency to the system.” APPPOA Information Secretary Nadeem Aziz Khan also addressed the press conference, saying that the daily fixing of fuel prices was to happen on a trial basis. He said that the petroleum minister had promised that the government would fix the prices daily “for two weeks on a trial basis” and review after a fortnight the “merits and demerits” that it brought. “If it does not cause much difficulty for people, then we will continue it. Otherwise, if there are a lot of difficulties, it will be reconsidered … and we will bring it back to our old routine of (fixing prices) every month or on a fortnightly basis again,” he cited Malik as saying. The promise, which the secretary said was also made official in writing, came after a “detailed session” with the petroleum minister today discussing the issues faced by the APPPOA. Khan added that Malik promised to sort out these issues within two weeks, thanking him for having taken the association on board and given them his time. Upon the minister’s assurance, he said, the association was delaying its shutdown call. He expressed his thanks to all the stakeholders that showed commitment to standing with the APPPOA and the dealers in Pakistan for their support. Khan also noted that the association had made no illegitimate demands, but was demanding a legitimate right that had been halted since 2022. He expressed his hope that the petroleum minister would fulfill his commitments. After yesterday’s meeting with the government, APPPOA representatives said their hour-long talks over the fuel pricing mechanism had “ failed ” to reach an agreement and that they were left with no option but to go on an indefinite strike, calling it an issue of survival. Speaking to the media, Vice Chairman Nauman Ali Butt said the government’s plan to revise petroleum prices on a daily basis was “unacceptable to petrol pump dealers and unworkable”. He said their main demand was that fuel prices should be determined on a monthly basis, adding that their longstanding demand for an increase in dealer commission had also remained pending, as the commission had remained frozen for the past three years.

items.title: Ejiofor hails NBA President-elect Badejo-Okunsanya, says victory marks beginning of greater task
items.description: By Nwabueze Okonkwo ONITSHA — Human rights lawyer, Sir Ifeanyi Ejiofor, has congratulated Mrs. Oyinkansola Badejo-Okunsanya, SAN, on her emergence as the 33rd President of the Nigerian Bar Association (NBA), saying her election victory marks the beginning of a greater responsibility. In a statement issued on Wednesday titled “The Ballot Has Spoken: A New Chapter for the Nigerian Bar,” Ejiofor said the conclusion of the election should pave the way for unity, reconciliation and renewed focus on repositioning the legal profession. He congratulated Badejo-Okunsanya, describing her emergence as the product of a keenly contested election and a sustained engagement with members of the profession across the country. “Since the ballot has spoken and the contest is over, it is now time for the NBA to move beyond the rhetoric, recriminations and bruised emotions that inevitably accompany every fiercely contested election,” he said. Ejiofor acknowledged that disagreements, complaints and criticisms were common features of democratic processes, particularly within a professional body such as the NBA, but urged members to accept the outcome and work towards strengthening the association. He noted that while the three Senior Advocates who contested for the presidency brought their visions, experience and competence to the race, only one candidate could emerge victorious. “Three candidates may contest; three may inspire; three may deserve admiration; but three cannot, by the very logic of electoral democracy, emerge President at the same time,” he said. The lawyer called on members of the Bar to lower the temperature of post-election discussions and give the incoming administration the opportunity to discharge its responsibilities. He also advised senior members of the profession to exercise restraint in airing grievances publicly, stressing that constructive criticism remained necessary for institutional growth but should not diminish the dignity of the legal profession. “The Bar does not need to turn every disagreement into a television series, nor should every electoral grievance be given a permanent residency in the studios of every available media platform,” he said. Addressing the President-elect, Ejiofor urged Badejo-Okunsanya to be magnanimous in victory and extend a hand of fellowship to other contestants. He said those who contested against her should not be regarded as defeated enemies but as valuable partners in advancing the NBA. “The election may have produced one President, but it must not produce a divided Bar. The Bar must evolve, but it must evolve without losing its soul,” he added. Ejiofor, however, charged the incoming NBA leadership to address critical challenges facing lawyers, including the declining economic viability of legal practice, welfare of young lawyers, professional opportunities, access to justice, independence of the profession and public confidence in the justice system. He expressed hope that Badejo-Okunsanya’s administration would be guided by courage, inclusiveness and reforms aimed at strengthening the association. “Election victory is only the beginning. The Bar now awaits the legacy,” he said. Ejiofor further described the new NBA president’s mandate as an opportunity to deliver meaningful transformation, adding that the campaign had ended and the responsibility of leadership had begun. The post Ejiofor hails NBA President-elect Badejo-Okunsanya, says victory marks beginning of greater task appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: N12.8trn SWV: APC’s panic exposes guilty conscience — Atiku
items.description: By Omeiza Ajayi, Abuja Former Vice President Atiku Abubakar has accused the ruling All Progressives Congress APC of dodging accountability over a ₦12.8 trillion Service-Wide Vote SWV in the 2026 Appropriation Act, saying the ruling party’s angry reaction to questions about the allocation betrays a guilty conscience rather than confidence in its own budget. APC had, in a statement on Wednesday, said Atiku had become more disoriented and misguided than ever before in the history of his presidential ambitions, adding that he advanced no facts to support his claim of malfeasance beyond conjecture calculated to inflame and mislead Nigerians. However, Atiku’s strategic media associate, Olusola Sanni, said in a counter on Wednesday that the presidential candidate of the African Democratic Congress ADC was not contesting the legitimacy of the service-wide vote as a budgetary tool but demanding answers on why the figure has surged by roughly 1,918 per cent in two years, from ₦638.34 billion in 2024 to its current level. At the heart of the dispute is a question Sanni said the APC’s National Publicity Secretary, Felix Morka, avoided entirely in his earlier defence of the administration: what unforeseen emergency could justify an increase of that scale within such a short period? “What emergency or unforeseen expenditure justifies increasing the service-wide vote from ₦638.34 billion in 2024 to over ₦12.8 trillion in 2026? That is an increase of approximately 1,918 per cent in barely two years,” Wanna said, dismissing the APC’s contingency-fund defence as a “lazy excuse”. Sanni argued that an allocation of that magnitude now rivals the combined budgets of major sectors, including defence, education, healthcare and works, and said the absence of clearly named projects under the vote deepens suspicion that it has drifted from its original purpose. “A contingency fund without specificity, accountability or transparency ceases to be a contingency provision and becomes precisely what Nigerians fear—a slush fund waiting to be abused,” he said. The statement also linked the current dispute to a pattern of budget controversies it said has trailed the Bola Tinubu administration, including duplicated line items, unclear religious project allocations and unnamed palace renovation spending, all of which it claimed have weakened public trust in the federal appropriation process. Sanni said the APC’s response to these questions had so far consisted of personal attacks rather than figures, insisting that no amount of insults from the party could substitute for a proper account of how the money would be spent. Atiku renewed his call for the Tinubu administration to publish a full breakdown of the ₦12.8 trillion vote, naming every project, beneficiary, implementing agency and the legal basis for each expenditure, warning that until this is done, Nigerians would conclude that the APC’s outrage stems from an inability to answer the questions rather than any weakness in them. The post N12.8trn SWV: APC’s panic exposes guilty conscience — Atiku appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Mort d’Abderrahim Faqir à Bologne : autopsie prévue vendredi, l’absence de soins au cœur de l’enquête
items.description: De nouveaux éléments émergent dans l’enquête sur la mort d’Abderrahim Faqir, ressortissant marocain de 42 ans décédé dimanche 19 juillet lors d’une intervention de la police à Bologne, en Italie. Selon des médias italiens, la justice cherche notamment à déterminer si la victime a reçu une assistance médicale appropriée après son immobilisation. Une autopsie doit être pratiquée vendredi afin de préciser les causes exactes du décès. D’après le quotidien italien Il Resto del Carlino, le parquet de Bologne s’intéresse dans un premier temps aux conditions d’intervention des secours. Les enquêteurs chercheraient notamment à comprendre pourquoi aucun médecin ni infirmier n’a été dépêché sur les lieux après un appel adressé au service d’urgence italien 118. Six personnes présentes lors des faits seraient concernées par les premières investigations : deux policiers et quatre secouristes. L’enquête devrait notamment examiner les raisons pour lesquelles les services d’urgence ont envoyé une équipe de volontaires sans médecin ni infirmier, alors que la police avait signalé une personne présentée comme étant en situation de crise psychologique. Les conditions de prise en charge examinées La question de l’assistance apportée à Abderrahim Faqir après son immobilisation constitue l’un des principaux volets de l’enquête. Selon l’avocat représentant la famille, des vidéos de la scène montreraient qu’après que les policiers ont cessé d’immobiliser le ressortissant marocain et se sont relevés, aucune assistance immédiate ne lui aurait été apportée. La défense de la famille estime qu’il pourrait encore avoir été en vie à ce moment-là, une hypothèse qui devra être vérifiée par les investigations et les conclusions médico-légales. Les enquêteurs disposent de plusieurs éléments vidéo pour tenter de reconstituer précisément le déroulement des faits. Outre les images diffusées sur Internet, ils auraient récupéré des séquences filmées par des témoins, des enregistrements provenant de caméras de surveillance installées dans le secteur ainsi que les images de la caméra-piéton portée par l’un des policiers. L’autopsie du corps d’Abderrahim Faqir, annoncée pour vendredi par l’avocat de sa famille, devrait constituer une étape déterminante pour établir les causes et les circonstances précises de sa mort. L’intervention de la police également passée au crible Les enquêteurs s’intéressent également aux méthodes employées par les policiers lors de l’intervention. Parmi les éléments examinés figurent notamment la distance à laquelle du spray au poivre aurait été utilisé et les éventuels effets de ce produit sur l’état de santé de la victime. L’avocat des policiers, Gabriele Bordoni, affirme pour sa part que l’opération d’immobilisation aurait été menée conformément au protocole en vigueur. Il soutient également qu’aucune pression n’aurait été exercée sur le corps d’Abderrahim Faqir à l’aide des genoux. Selon La Repubblica, le parquet de Bologne concentrerait ainsi ses investigations sur deux axes principaux : d’une part, les procédures suivies par le service italien d’urgence médicale 118 et, d’autre part, le respect par les policiers des protocoles prévus lors de l’immobilisation d’une personne en état de forte agitation. Les enquêteurs continueraient parallèlement à rechercher d’autres vidéos susceptibles d’avoir été filmées par des habitants du quartier afin de disposer de la chronologie la plus complète possible des événements. Le Maroc réclame une enquête approfondie L’affaire a également provoqué une réaction officielle des autorités marocaines. Le ministère des Affaires étrangères, de la Coopération africaine et des Marocains résidant à l’étranger a indiqué suivre avec une « grande préoccupation » les circonstances ayant conduit à la mort du ressortissant marocain à Bologne. Les autorités marocaines ont demandé à leurs homologues italiennes d’accélérer la conduite d’une enquête complète afin de faire toute la lumière sur les circonstances du décès et de fournir les explications nécessaires. Rabat a également insisté sur la nécessité de déterminer les éventuelles responsabilités et de garantir l’application de l’ensemble des procédures et mesures prévues par la loi. De son côté, le Club des avocats au Maroc a annoncé avoir déposé une plainte-signalement auprès du parquet du tribunal de Bologne à la suite du décès d’Abderrahim Faqir. Âgé de 42 ans, le ressortissant marocain est décédé lors d’une intervention menée par des policiers italiens, dimanche 19 juillet, dans le quartier Pilastro à Bologne. Les résultats de l’autopsie et l’exploitation des différents enregistrements vidéo devraient désormais permettre aux enquêteurs de mieux déterminer la chronologie des faits et d’établir les circonstances exactes de sa mort. The post Mort d’Abderrahim Faqir à Bologne : autopsie prévue vendredi, l’absence de soins au cœur de l’enquête appeared first on Hespress Français - Actualités du Maroc .

items.title: Casamance : la boucle Nimzat–Kampila–RN4 inaugurée pour renforcer le désenclavement
items.description: Ce Mercredi 22 juillet 2026, le ministre de l’Urbanisme, des Collectivités territoriales et de l’Aménagement du territoire, Moussa Bala Fofana, accompagné de Mame Sophietou Djamil Gueye, chargée de programme de la Banque mondiale, et du Directeur général de l’AGETIP, El Hadji Malick Gaye, a procédé à l’inauguration de la boucle de Nimzat – Kampila – RN4, dans la commune de Bona, d’une longueur de 14 km, réalisée dans le cadre du Programme de développement économique de la Casamance (PDEC).À cette occasion, le ministre s’est félicité de la qualité des travaux réalisés et a salué l’impact de cette infrastructure sur la mobilité des populations et le développement local. Ce projet d'aménagement et de désenclavement s'inscrit dans la dynamique nationale de modernisation des infrastructures rurales au Sénégal. En reliant directement les localités de Nimzat et de Kampila à la Route Nationale 4 (RN4), cette piste joue un rôle déterminant pour la mobilité des populations locales. Elle fluidifie le transport des personnes, sécurise les déplacements et facilite l'accès aux services sociaux de base comme la santé et l'éducation. Sur le plan économique, ce tronçon de 14 km constitue un levier majeur pour le développement agricole de la zone. Il permet aux producteurs d'acheminer plus rapidement et à moindre coût leurs récoltes vers les grands marchés régionaux, réduisant ainsi les pertes post-récolte. Géré sous la supervision des autorités étatiques, notamment à travers les programmes de pistes communautaires, cet axe routier participe activement au désenclavement de la Casamance et au renforcement de l'intégration économique locale. A.N

items.title: Van Bommel set to take over as Belgium coach – Reports
items.description: Former Netherlands midfielder Mark van Bommel is set to take over as coach of the Belgium national team, local media reported Wednesday. Van Bommel and the Belgian football federation (RBFA) have come to an agreement that will see the 49-year-old sign a contract in the coming days to take charge of the Red Devils through to Euro 2028, according to Flemish daily Het Laatste Nieuws. After hanging up his boots in 2013, Van Bommel has cut his teeth as coach of PSV Eindhoven, Wolfsburg and Antwerp. Van Bommel has been without a team since departing Belgian top-flight club Antwerp in June 2024, a year after having guided them to a first league title in 66 years. The 2010 World Cup finalist is set to take over from Rudi Garcia, who left his role on Monday in the wake of Belgium’s quarter-final exit against eventual winners Spain at this summer’s finals in North America. Local media reported differences of opinion between the French coach and several players, as well as with technical director Vincent Mannaert, who was unhappy with certain group-stage performances. Garcia’s departure, however, was met with bewilderment by many supporters on social media. According to Het Laatste Nieuws, the RBFA was looking for “a younger and more modern coach than Rudi Garcia (aged 62)”. AFP The post Van Bommel set to take over as Belgium coach – Reports appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: « Sale Arabe, sale bougnoule » : la disparition tragique de Joris Laurencin bouleverse la France
items.description: Dix jours après la disparition tragique de Joris Laurencin, jeune Franco-Algérien de 21 ans, qui s’est donné la mort, son oncle a pris la parole. Le drame s'est produit le 13 juillet. Le jeune homme a mis fin à ses jours avec la carabine de chasse de son père. Selon son entourage, ce geste de désespoir serait lié à des faits de harcèlement à caractère raciste en raison de ses origines algériennes du côté maternel. Ses parents ont déposé plainte pour harcèlement moral et provocation au suicide, une démarche qui a conduit à l'ouverture d'une seconde enquête. Parallèlement, un appel à témoins a été diffusé sur les réseaux sociaux. Joris Laurencin traité de « sale Arabe, sale bougnoule » Interrogé par France 3, l'oncle du jeune Franco-Algérien est revenu sur les difficultés qu'il aurait rencontrées depuis sa majorité. « Les propos qu'il rapportait à son père, c'était que ses copains se moquaient de lui. Ils l'appelaient sale Arabe, sale bougnoule, traître. Petit à petit, cela l'a complexé. Il disait qu'il n'aimait plus être mat de peau, être bronzé. Il souffrait de cette image qu'on lui renvoyait et il en parlait régulièrement à son père », a-t-il confié. Selon lui, un autre épisode aurait profondément marqué le jeune homme. Lors d'un événement festif, un tee-shirt portant l'inscription « DZ Pagu », en référence à ses origines algériennes selon ses proches, lui aurait été remis. « Il avait demandé un tee-shirt comme tout le monde. À la place, il s'est retrouvé avec un tee-shirt marqué DZ Pagu. Il est rentré chez lui très affecté. Avec son père, ils ont essayé d'effacer l'inscription au fer à repasser, puis son père a écrit « Jojo » au feutre à la place. C'est un souvenir qui les a profondément marqués », a raconté son oncle. Ce dernier affirme également qu'au retour de la foire de Saint-Jean-de-Bournay, organisée entre le 30 juin et le 2 juillet, Joris lui aurait rapporté avoir été victime de nouvelles insultes. « Ils m'ont pris pour un con, ils se sont foutus de moi, ils m'ont traité de bougnoule, de sale Arabe. Ils ont dit qu'ils allaient me planter », aurait-il déclaré avant de partir en courant. Un appel à témoins lancé par les proches Le 20 juillet, ses proches ont publié un appel à témoins sur les réseaux sociaux afin de recueillir d'éventuelles informations. « Faites la lumière sur ce qu'il s'est passé », peut-on lire dans le message. Ils recherchent notamment des personnes ayant assisté à des insultes, propos racistes, menaces ou tout autre incident impliquant Joris lors de la vogue de Saint-Jean-de-Bournay. Les détenteurs d'informations sont invités à prendre contact avec la famille ou avec les enquêteurs. « Aujourd'hui, notre famille est brisée. Nous avons perdu un fils, un petit-fils, un neveu, un cousin de seulement 21 ans », ont-ils écrit. « Même si cela vous semble sans importance, par respect pour sa mémoire et pour aider à faire toute la lumière sur ce qui s'est passé, nous vous demandons de parler. » Une lettre adressée à ses parents a également été retrouvée. « Papa, maman, je vous aime. J'ai toujours été gentil. Les gens sont très méchants avec moi », y est-il écrit. Le suicide de Joris Laurencin suscite une vive émotion en France Ce drame a suscité une forte émotion en France. Plusieurs responsables politiques ainsi que SOS Racisme ont exprimé leur soutien à la famille. Le chef de La France Insoumise (LFI), Jean-Luc Mélenchon, a réagi sur X : « Joris, 21 ans, s'est suicidé après avoir subi un harcèlement raciste de plusieurs années. Insultes, tabassage, mise à nu (…) Pensées pour la famille et les proches. Justice maintenant. » Joris, 21 ans, s’est suicidé après avoir subi un harcèlement raciste de plusieurs années. Insultes, tabassage, mise à nu… Ses harceleurs lui ont tout fait. La famille a lancé un appel à témoins pour recueillir des témoignages, cet article résume bien la situation. Je vous… — Jean-Luc Mélenchon (@JLMelenchon) July 20, 2026 La députée écologiste franco-algérienne Sabrina Sebaihi s'est également exprimée. « Oui, en France en 2026, le racisme tue. Et pendant que la haine se banalise autour de nous, certains continuent de dire que ce ne sont « que des mots ». (…) La haine ne reste jamais sans conséquences. » De son côté, le député Renaissance et ancien premier ministre Gabriel Attal a évoqué un harcèlement devenu « insoutenable ». SOS Racisme a également réagi : « face au harcèlement raciste, le silence protège les auteurs. Ce drame n'est pas un simple fait divers. » Selon un reportage de BFM Lyon, Joris était décrit par son entourage comme quelqu'un de « très souriant, très gentil » qui « ne montrait pas quand ça allait mal ». Deux enquêtes sont actuellement ouvertes par le parquet de Vienne. La première vise à déterminer les circonstances exactes du décès du jeune homme. La seconde porte sur les faits de harcèlement dénoncés par ses proches. L’article « Sale Arabe, sale bougnoule » : la disparition tragique de Joris Laurencin bouleverse la France est apparu en premier sur TSA .

items.title: Trump vows tit-for-tat strikes on Iran power plants
items.description: President Donald Trump on Wednesday pledged that the United States would bomb Iranian civil infrastructure every time Tehran fires at ships in the strategic Strait of Hormuz. "From this point forward, any time the Islamic Republic of Iran shoots at a ship in the Strait of Hormuz, whether it be by Missile, Rocket, Drone, or any other device or weapon, the United States will bomb and destroy ONE BRIDGE OR POWER PLANT," he wrote on his Truth Social network.

items.title: Africa can no longer rely on foreign aid for healthcare – Deputy Health Minister
items.description: Deputy Minister of Health, Professor Grace Ayensu-Danquah, has called on African countries to strengthen domestic health financing as external donor support becomes increasingly uncertain. She stressed that the continent must take greater responsibility for funding its healthcare priorities. Delivering a keynote address on behalf of Vice President Prof. Naana Jane Opoku-Agyemang at a High-Level Interactive Policy Dialogue in Accra on July 20, Prof. Ayensu-Danquah said Africa’s future health security depends on sustained local investment rather than reliance on development assistance. The dialogue, organised by the African Renaissance Trust and the Jakaya Mrisho Kikwete Foundation on the sidelines of the African Union Extraordinary Summit, focused on advancing domestic health financing, accountability and strategic innovation across the continent. Reflecting on the Abuja Declaration, adopted 25 years ago, which committed African countries to allocating at least 15 per cent of their national budgets to health, the Deputy Minister said the continent must honestly assess its progress amid changing global financing trends. “Official development assistance is less certain. Long-standing funding models are changing. Programmes that many health systems have relied on can no longer be taken for granted,” she noted. “That reality means Africa must strengthen its ability to finance its own priorities, build enduring institutions, and invest in health not as a recurring expense, but as a foundation for economic growth and human development.” Prof. Ayensu-Danquah said the government of Ghana is pursuing universal health coverage through policies aimed at expanding access to healthcare, improving health outcomes, and reducing out-of-pocket expenditure. She highlighted two flagship initiatives introduced by the government, the Ghana Medical Trust Fund (MahamaCares) and the Free Primary Healthcare Initiative, as key interventions designed to address financing gaps in the country’s health sector. According to her, the Ghana Medical Trust Fund provides financial support for the diagnosis, treatment and long-term management of chronic non-communicable diseases that are either inadequately covered or not covered under the National Health Insurance Scheme (NHIS). “Beyond supporting patient care, the fund also contributes to strengthening specialist healthcare by investing in medical infrastructure, equipment, research and specialist training,” she said. On the Free Primary Healthcare Initiative, she explained that the programme complements the NHIS by making essential primary healthcare services more accessible, with a strong emphasis on disease prevention. Under the initiative, the Minister said that every Ghanaian will be entitled to routine health screening for conditions including hypertension, diabetes and certain cancers, while also benefiting from maternal and child healthcare services, immunisation, treatment of common illnesses, community outreach, home visits and public health education. The programme, she said, is currently being rolled out in phases, beginning with 150 underserved districts, with nationwide coverage expected over the next few years. Prof. Ayensu-Danquah also announced that the government has made a $50 million seed investment in the National Vaccine Institute to strengthen local vaccine production and pharmaceutical manufacturing. She said the investment builds on Ghana’s growing regulatory capacity, including the Food and Drugs Authority’s World Health Organization Maturity Level 3 status and progress towards the commercial production of locally manufactured anti-snake venom. The Deputy Minister welcomed the unveiling of the African Renaissance Health Financing Insights Dashboard, describing it as an important accountability tool that will enable governments to better track health financing commitments and outcomes across the continent. “I thank the African Renaissance Trust for presenting this work and look forward to the discussions it will generate among governments, continental institutions and development partners,” she said. She stressed that strengthening Africa’s health systems goes beyond financing alone and requires increased investment in pharmaceutical manufacturing, research, innovation, workforce development and stronger continental institutions. Concluding her address, Prof. Ayensu-Danquah urged African governments to honour their commitments by translating policy declarations into measurable improvements in people’s lives. “Our declarations must be translated into measurable progress in the lives of our people,” she said. She reaffirmed Ghana’s commitment to working with the African Union, regional institutions and development partners to build stronger, more resilient and self-reliant health systems capable of meeting the continent’s future needs.

items.title: Tombolia Plateau 1 : le Groupe SONOCO remet un bureau de quartier et du matériel d’assainissement
items.description: Le Groupe SONOCO a procédé, mercredi 22 juillet 2026, à la remise officielle d’un bureau entièrement construit et aménagé au profit du Comité de voisinage de Tombolia Plateau 1. À cette occasion, le Groupe a également offert du matériel destiné aux opérations d’assainissement du quartier et annoncé la prise en charge de la logistique nécessaire à l’évacuation des déchets. La cérémonie s’est déroulée en présence de M. Cheick Mohamed Cissé, Maire de la Commune urbaine de Tombolia, de M. Ibrahima Kanfory Sylla, Chef de quartier, des représentants du Comité de voisinage ainsi que de plusieurs dirigeants et collaborateurs du Groupe SONOCO. Le Directeur exécutif Groupe, les Directeurs généraux de LMA – Les Moulins d’Afrique et de la Compagnie des boissons africaines (CBA), le Directeur HSE Groupe ainsi que le représentant du Directeur général d’Africa Food Industries (AFI) ont notamment pris part à l’événement. Un cadre de proximité au service des habitants Le nouveau bâtiment permettra aux responsables du quartier et aux membres du Comité de voisinage de disposer d’un cadre adapté pour recevoir les habitants, organiser leurs réunions et assurer un meilleur suivi des préoccupations de la communauté. Prenant la parole au nom du Groupe SONOCO, Mme Soumah Aminata Rabi Condé, Directrice Communication et RSE, a rappelé la vocation de cet espace. « Nous souhaitons que ce bureau soit un véritable lieu d’écoute, de dialogue et de recherche de solutions au service des populations. À travers cette réalisation, notre message est simple et fort : le Groupe SONOCO est à vos côtés. » Une réalisation saluée par les autorités communales Dans son allocution, M. Cheick Mohamed Cissé, installé à la tête de la Commune urbaine de Tombolia depuis moins d’un mois, a exprimé sa joie et sa fierté de prendre part à cette cérémonie. Il a souligné que cette initiative allait bien au-delà d’une simple remise de bâtiment et traduisait la convergence entre la vision du Groupe SONOCO en matière de développement communautaire et celle portée par les autorités locales au bénéfice des populations. « Cette cérémonie est pour moi un motif de joie, de fierté et de réconfort. Loin d’être une simple remise de bâtiment, elle symbolise la rencontre entre la vision du Groupe SONOCO et le projet de développement que nous souhaitons porter au sein de notre collectivité », a-t-il déclaré. Le Maire a remercié le Groupe SONOCO pour son engagement auprès des communautés de Tombolia, réaffirmé le soutien de la collectivité aux initiatives contribuant à l’amélioration du cadre de vie et rassuré quant à la bonne utilisation et à l’entretien du bâtiment. Un appui concret aux opérations d’assainissement En réponse à une sollicitation des responsables locaux concernant le nettoyage des caniveaux, le Groupe SONOCO a remis au quartier des brouettes, des râteaux et des pelles. Il prendra également en charge les frais logistiques liés au transport et à l’évacuation des déchets collectés. Cette double initiative s’inscrit dans la politique de responsabilité sociétale du Groupe SONOCO, articulée autour de cinq axes prioritaires : l’éducation, la santé, l’emploi, les infrastructures et l’environnement. « Depuis plus de vingt ans, le Groupe SONOCO mène régulièrement des actions au bénéfice des communautés riveraines de ses différents sites. Nous restons engagés à accompagner les initiatives qui contribuent concrètement à l’amélioration des conditions de vie des populations », a souligné Mme Soumah Aminata Rabi Condé. Une collaboration saluée par la communauté Dans leur intervention, les représentants du Comité de voisinage ont salué la qualité du dialogue établi avec le Groupe SONOCO et les différentes réalisations menées au bénéfice de Tombolia Plateau 1. Ils ont notamment évoqué la distribution de kits scolaires, la rénovation d’une mosquée, la réalisation de trois forages dans les secteurs Industrie, Rails et Samourayah, ainsi que la construction et l’aménagement du nouveau bureau de quartier. Le Chef de quartier, M. Ibrahima Kanfory Sylla, a exprimé la reconnaissance de la communauté et souligné l’importance de ce nouvel espace pour le fonctionnement des instances locales et le traitement des préoccupations des habitants. « Ce nouveau bureau constitue une avancée importante pour notre quartier. Il nous permettra de mieux recevoir les citoyens, de renforcer le dialogue et de travailler plus efficacement à la résolution de leurs préoccupations. Nous remercions le Groupe SONOCO pour son écoute et pour les actions concrètes réalisées au bénéfice des populations de Tombolia Plateau 1. » La cérémonie s’est achevée par la remise symbolique des clés du bâtiment et du matériel d’assainissement, marquant une nouvelle étape dans le partenariat entre le Groupe SONOCO, les autorités locales et les communautés de Tombolia.

items.title: Osun NURTW crisis deepens as faction takes over secretariat
items.description: By Shina Abubakar, Osogbo The leadership crisis in the Osun State chapter of the National Union of Road Transport Workers (NURTW) has taken a new turn, as a faction loyal to Kazeem Oyewale has taken over the union’s state secretariat despite an order suspending its activities. Oyewale, popularly known as Asiri Eniba and a member of the All Progressives Congress (APC), took control of the secretariat located in the Ijetu area of Osogbo from Wakili Nurudeen, also known as Alowonle, a member of the Accord Party, who had claimed a legal mandate to remain in office. The development followed a violent clash between rival factions after supporters of the Eniba-led group staged a protest, disrupting business activities in the state capital and leading to the arrest of some suspects. Following the unrest, chairmen of local government areas in the state announced the suspension of NURTW activities over rising security concerns. The suspension affected operations of the union and motorcycle riders’ union across the state, with reports of gunshots in parts of Ilesa and Ile-Ife on Tuesday. In a video circulating on social media, Oyewale was seen entering the union’s secretariat with his aides, inspecting offices and the main hall. Speaking on the development, Oyewale confirmed that he had resumed office, saying the takeover was peaceful and followed a vote of no confidence passed against Alowonle by members. “Yes, it is true that I have taken over. As I speak with you, I am in the office,” he said. Efforts to get a reaction from Nurudeen were unsuccessful, as he did not respond to calls placed to his phone. The post Osun NURTW crisis deepens as faction takes over secretariat appeared first on Vanguard News .
